hypothesis.H1: SIG-1 (node generalization) documented but not operationalized: 0 tools use NODES.md
hypothesis.H2: SIG-2 (structured signals) system exists but is write-only: <30% signal resolution rate
hypothesis.H3: Both signals should be PARTIALLY RESOLVED, not RESOLVED or OPEN

method: Systematic codebase audit: grep for hardcoded 'human' references, check NODES.md imports, measure signal resolution rates, trace signal processing pipeline

specific_gaps: No node registry (runtime data structure mapping node_id to properties)
maintenance.py gates HIGH-RISK lanes on human_open_item (not capability-based)
HUMAN-QUEUE.md still checked separately from SIGNALS.md
No ai_open_item or node_open_item equivalent in lane contracts
No signal-to-action routing pipeline (sense without act)
Directive signals at priority 5 (lowest) in swarm_cycle.py
SIG-2 itself was open 56 sessions (recursive dysfunction)
Domain SIGNALS.md files abandoned (5 empty files)
No auto-detection of when signal work is complete
No escalation mechanism for aging signals

fixes_applied.orient_py_signal_due: P1 signals >30 sessions old now surface as SIGNAL-DUE in suggested action section
fixes_applied.orient_py_partial_resolved_tracking: PARTIALLY RESOLVED signals now included in signal backlog count
fixes_applied.sig1_sig2_resolution_correction: Downgraded from premature RESOLVED to PARTIALLY RESOLVED with evidence

conclusion: SIG-1 and SIG-2 represent a specification-implementation gap: the swarm documented the solution (NODES.md v1.0, swarm_signal.py) but never operationalized it in tools. 0/207 tools reference NODES.md. Signal resolution rate is 17.5%. The gap between designing a system and wiring it into the workflow is the root failure. L-601 applies: measurement/specification without structural enforcement decays to documentation.
falsification: Falsified if: SIGNAL-DUE mechanism produces 0 signal resolutions in 10 sessions
